examples: We use thymol-sodium hypobromite as chromogenic reagent.
In this test, an aldehyde is treated with Tollens' reagent.
The ion-supported hypervalent iodine(iii) reagent is a kind of "task-specific ionic liquids".
Inside the chambers the sample mixes with a freeze-dried bead of a chemical reagent designed for a particular test.
It had developed only eight reagent beads--enough for glucose and liver function, for example, but not enough to measure chloride or potassium.
